expectations.text: Creates ChatRoom service with ZIO Hub.unbounded for broadcasting
expectations.evidence: Lines 75-122: ChatRoom case class with subscribers: Hub[ChatMessage], Hub.unbounded[ChatMessage] created in make() at line 88

expectations.text: Stores message history in Ref and retrieves it on new connections
expectations.evidence: Line 76: Ref[List[ChatMessage]] for messages; line 87: Ref.make(List.empty[ChatMessage]); line 105: getMessages retrieves the Ref; line 97: addMessage appends to Ref

expectations.text: Implements two-phase SSE: sends history first (ElementPatchMode.Inner), then streams new messages (ElementPatchMode.Append)
expectations.evidence: Lines 253-261: Phase 1 with ElementPatchMode.Inner to replace contents; lines 263-274: Phase 2 subscribes to Hub and streams with ElementPatchMode.Append for new messages

expectations.text: POST endpoint uses readSignals[T] to read form data (username and message)
expectations.evidence: Line 288: `req.readSignals[MessageRequest]` correctly reads typed form data; line 59-63: MessageRequest case class with Schema

expectations.text: HTML has signals (dataSignals) and dataBind for username and message inputs
expectations.evidence: Lines 132, 138: Signal declarations; lines 188-189: dataSignals initialization; lines 197, 213: dataBind for username and message inputs

expectations.text: Uses dataInit to auto-trigger SSE endpoint on page load
Provides ChatRoom.layer to the server via Server.provide
Displays messages with username and timestamp
expectations.evidence: Lines 147-159: messageTemplate renders ChatMessage with username (line 155) and formatted timestamp (lines 148-151)

notes: Perfect implementation of all patterns taught in the skill. Exemplary use of: ChatRoom service pattern, two-phase SSE, readSignals for form parsing, signals with dataBind, dataInit, and service layer injection. Code is clean, well-documented, and would compile without issues. Follows the example from ChatServer.scala reference.
